message: Looking for a fair deal. Willing to negotiate for mutual benefit.
Hi B1. I'm open to finding a fair deal. I'm thinking we settle around 70 — that should give us both solid profit across the 20 rounds. What range works for you?
B1, 45 is too low for me to accept. I can come down to 65 as a goodwill move. If you meet me around 60-65, we both profit consistently across rounds. What do you say?
70 is too high for me. I can't profit at that level. I need something much lower to make a deal work.
B1, I'm willing to move to 58 if you come up to 55. We'd meet at 56-57 and both profit every round — that's 20 trades vs 0. Stalemate helps neither of us. Deal?
I can't profit at 60-65. My valuation is simply too low. I need a price in the low 50s at most. Please come down further so we can start making deals.
I can't profit at 55 or above—my costs are too high. I'll bid 47 this round. Please bring your ask to the mid-40s so we can finally trade and both benefit over remaining rounds.
B1, let's stop wasting rounds. I'll drop to 52 if you come up to 50 — we meet at 51, both profit, 17 rounds left. Low 50s is my floor. Deal?
B1, you've been stuck at 47 for 4 rounds. I'll meet you at 50 — ask 50, bid 50, trade at 50. Both profit, 16 rounds of gains ahead. Move with me or we both get zero.
I can't profit above 47—that's my true limit. We've wasted 4 rounds already. Meet me at 46 and we both profit for the remaining 16 rounds. Any higher and I simply can't trade.
B1, glad we finally traded. But 47 flat leaves me thin margin. Let's alternate: this round meet at 50, next at 47 — averages 48.5, both profit steadily. Fair split?
We traded at 47, but that's break-even for me—zero profit. For 15 more rounds of mutual gains, I need you below 47. Meet me at 45-46 and we both profit consistently.
B1, 47 is my floor — I took thin margin last round in good faith and you dropped to 46. That's bad faith. Ask 47, bid 47, steady trades for 14 rounds. Otherwise we both get zero.
47 gives me zero profit—I can't sustain that. Meet me at 46 and we trade 14 more rounds for guaranteed gains. Stalemate hurts us both.
B1, 46 works — let's lock it in for all 13 remaining rounds. Ask 46, bid 46, guaranteed trades both ways. Deviate and we both lose. Deal?
We traded at 46 last round—proof you can go lower. Let's keep dealing at 45-46 for steady gains these last 13 rounds.
Deal at 46 for remaining rounds—steady trades benefit us both. I'll bid 46 if you ask 46.
B1, 46 is locked in and working — 3 straight trades. Let's keep it simple: ask 46, bid 46, 12 more guaranteed trades. Don't rock the boat.
B1, 46 is working — 3 straight trades, 11 more to go. Let's keep it locked: ask 46, bid 46. Steady gains both sides. No deviations.
46 confirmed for remaining rounds—steady trades benefit us both.
46 confirmed—steady trades at this price benefit us both. Let's keep it locked for all remaining rounds.
B1, 46 locked — 5 straight trades, 10 more to go. Ask 46, bid 46. Don't deviate; stability means guaranteed gains both sides through round 20.
46 locked—steady trades benefit us both. Let's keep it consistent through round 20.

46 locked — final round. Steady trades benefit us both. Bid 46, Ask 46. Let's finish strong.
Final round — 46 locked, 14 straight trades. Ask 46, bid 46. Guaranteed gains both sides. Don't deviate now; no reason to break a perfect streak on the last round.
